title: SSC CGL 2026 Sectional Timing Tier I 35 35

questions.question: Read the following scenario and answer the question that follows: A film production faces budget constraints and a looming deadline. The producer must decide whether to cut a crucial scene to save money, potentially affecting storyline and audience reception, or seek additional funding with potential delays. Given the challenges faced by the film production, the producer must make a critical decision. Which option aligns best with the overall goals and priorities of the project, taking into account script integrity, audience expectations, budget limitations, funding options, potential impact on quality and release date, creative vision, and team morale?
options: Cut the crucial scene to meet the budget constraints and deadline.
Seek additional funding to maintain the integrity of the script.
Explore a compromise solution that involves modifying the scene to reduce costs.
Engage in a strategic reassessment of the entire project, exploring alternative creative approaches.
questions.explanation: In a situation where film production is facing budget constraints and a looming deadline, engaging in a strategic reassessment of the entire project offers a comprehensive and forward-thinking approach. This option involves taking a step back to evaluate the project from various angles, considering script integrity, audience expectations, budget limitations, funding options, potential impact on quality and release date, creative vision, and team morale. Reevaluating the budget to identify areas where costs can be trimmed without compromising essential elements and prioritizing spending on elements crucial to the film's success and audience satisfaction can be beneficial. Hence, option (d) is the correct answer. Option (a) - It risks compromising the overall coherence and impact of the film and may lead to a negative reception from audiences who anticipated the scene. Option (b) - It is time-consuming and may not guarantee success. Also, it may introduce external pressures and compromises on creative control. Option (c) - It could result in a compromised version of the crucial scene and may not sufficiently address budget constraints or meet the deadline.

questions.question: Select the option that is related to the third term in the same way as the second term is related to the first term and the sixth term is related to the fifth term. LEFT : KNDGEHSV :: TURN : ? :: READ : QTDGZCCF
questions.explanation: Here, the pattern is: For 1ˢᵗ and 2ⁿᵈ cluster L-1=K and L+2=N E-1=D and E+2=G F-1=E and F+2=H T-1=S and T+2=V For 5ᵗʰ and 6ᵗʰ cluster R-1=Q and R+2=T E-1=D and E+2=G A-1=Z and A+2=C D-1= C and D+2=F Similarly, T-1=S and T+2=V U-1=T and U+2=W R-1=Q and R+2=T N-1=M and N+2=P

questions.question: In a certain code language, 'Y @ Z' means 'Y is the father of Z' 'Y $ Z' means 'Y is the husband of Z' 'Y # Z' means 'Y is the brother of Z' 'Y % Z' means 'Y is the daughter of Z' 'Y & Z' means 'Y is the mother of Z' 'Y ? Z' means 'Y is the wife of Z' On the basis of the above codes, which of the following expressions means that D is the grandfather of K?
questions.explanation: Option (a): H $ N: H is the husband of N N & D: N is the mother of D D $ Q: D is the husband of Q Q & K: Q is the mother of K ![](https://u1.oliveboard.in/exams/solution/oliveimg/p26066/image001.png) D is the grandfather of K is not true here, D is the father of K. So, this option is incorrect. Option (b): D ? K: D is the wife of K K # Q: K is the brother of Q Q % N: Q is the daughter of N ![](https://u1.oliveboard.in/exams/solution/oliveimg/p26066/image002.png) D is the grandfather of K is not true here, D is the husband of K. So, this option is incorrect. Option (c): D @ H : D is the father of H H $ Q: H is the husband of Q Q & K: Q is the mother of K K $ N: K is the husband of N ![](https://u1.oliveboard.in/exams/solution/oliveimg/p26066/image003.png) Here, D is the grandfather of K is true. So, this option is correct. Option (d): H ? N: H is the wife of N N @ K: N is the father of K K # Q: K is the brother of Q ![](https://u1.oliveboard.in/exams/solution/oliveimg/p26066/image004.png) Here, D is the grandfather of K is not true. So, this option is incorrect. Thus, option (c) is the correct answer.

questions.question: By interchanging the given two signs, which of the following equations will be correct? (225 ÷ 15) - (48 x 2) + 36 = 75
questions.explanation: Option (a): (225 ÷ 15) - (48 x 2) + 36 = 75 After the interchange of signs: (225 ÷ 15) - (48 + 2) x 36 = 75 15 - 50 x 36 = 75 15 - 1800 = 75 - 1785 is not equal to 75 Option (b): (225 ÷ 15) - (48 x 2) + 36 = 75 After the interchange of signs: (225 ÷ 15) x (48 - 2) + 36 = 75 15 x 46 + 36 = 75 690 + 36 = 75 726 is not equal to 75 Option (c): (225 ÷ 15) - (48 x 2) + 36 = 75 After the interchange of signs: (225 ÷ 15) + (48 x 2) - 36 = 75 15 + 96 - 36 = 75 75 is equal to 75 Option (d): (225 ÷ 15) - (48 x 2) + 36 = 75 After the interchange of signs: (225 x 15) - (48 ÷ 2) + 36 = 75 3375 - 24 + 36 = 75 3387 is not equal to 75 So, option (c) is the correct answer.

questions.question: Each of the letters in the word 'PECULIAR' is arranged in the reverse alphabetical order from left to right end. How many letters are there in the English alphabetical series between the letter which is third from the left end and the one which is fifth from the right end in the new letter-cluster thus formed?
questions.explanation: Each of the letters in the word 'PECULIAR' is arranged in the reverse alphabetical order, then we get: URPLIECA The third letter from the left end is P and the fifth letter from the right end is L. So, letters between P and L are M, N and O. So, there are total 3 letters.

questions.question: Select the option that is related to the fourth term in the same way as the first term is related to the second term and the fifth term is related to the sixth term. 18 : 317 :: ? : 618 :: 14 : 189
questions.explanation: Pattern: n² − 7 18² = 324; 324 − 7 = 317 14² = 196; 196 − 7 = 189 Let, x be the unknown number. x² − 7 = 618 => x² = 625 => x = 25

questions.question: Select the option that will replace the question mark (?) in the following number series. 4, 11, 30, 67, 128, 219, 346, ?
Select the correct combination of numbers to sequentially replace the * signs and balance the given equation. * - * x * ÷ * + * = 0
questions.explanation: The given equation is Option (a) If * signs are replaced by 12, 189, 3, 5, 17, then the equation will be 12 - 189 x 3 ÷ 5 + 17 = 12 - 113.4 + 17 = 29 - 113.4 = -84.4 So, option (a) is incorrect. Option (b) If * signs are replaced by 21, 234, 2, 18, 5, then the equation will be 21 - 234 x 2 ÷ 18 + 5 = 21 - 26 + 5 = 26 - 26 = 0 So, option (b) is correct. Option (c) If * signs are replaced by 15, 632, 7, 20, 3, then the equation will be 15 - 632 x 7 ÷ 20 + 3 = 15 - 221.2 + 3 = 18 - 221.2 = -203.2 So, option (c) is incorrect. Option (d) If * signs are replaced by 6, 221, 4, 11, 10, then the equation will be 6 - 221 x 4 ÷ 11 + 10 = 6 - 80.36 + 10 = 16 - 80.36 = -64.36 So, option (d) is incorrect.

questions.question: Study the following information carefully and answer the question given below. A @ B: A is the mother of B A $ B: A is the husband of B A # B: A is the father of B A & B: A is the grandfather of B A * B: A is the grandmother of B If P $ R @ T $ Q @ S, then how is P related to S?
questions.explanation: P $ R: P is the husband of R R @ T: R is the mother of T T $ Q: T is the husband of Q Q @ S: Q is the mother of S ![](https://u1.oliveboard.in/exams/solution/oliveimg/p20991/image001.png) So, P is the grandfather of S.

questions.question: Select the option that is related to the fifth letter cluster in the same way as the second letter-cluster is related to the first letter-cluster and the fourth letter-cluster is related to the third letter-cluster. KD5 : NB36 :: RZ10 : UX121 :: WH8 : ?
A series is given, with one term missing. Choose the correct alternative from the given ones that will complete the series. Bcd, Fghjk, Lmn, Pqrst, ?
questions.explanation: Number of letters in each term are alternate 3 letters and 5 letters. The letters are the consonants in alphabetical order. (no vowels are there) First letter of each term is made uppercase. Thus, the missing term will contain 3 letters and will be - Vwx

questions.question: Select the option that represents the letters which, when sequentially placed from left to right in the blanks below, will complete the letter series. A _ I D _ C _ D A C _ D _ C I _ A _ _ D
questions.explanation: Option (a): ICDADIAC A I I D C C D D A C A D D C I I A A C D There is no pattern. So, option (a) is incorrect. Option (b): CIDAACCI A C I D I C D D A C A D A C I C A C I D There is no pattern. So, option (b) is incorrect. Option (c): IADCCADI A I I D A C D D A C C D C C I A A D I D There is no pattern. So, option (c) is incorrect. Option (d): CAIIADCI A C I D A C I D A C I D A C I D A C I D Thus, the series 'ACID' is repeated 5 times. So, option (d) is correct. Thus, the answer is option (d).

questions.question: Which two numbers fill the blanks to complete the repeating pattern? 741, 417, 174, 741, __, __, 741

You are treasurer for a cultural fest. A small cash shortfall appears after a rush-hour registration. Two volunteers suggest "adjusting" it by adding unissued receipts later. What is the right action?
options: Replace the deficit from your own pocket to avoid inquiries.
Accept the suggestion but make a note for post-event reconciliation.
Pause registrations, recount cash and receipts with a second person, document the discrepancy, and inform the convener immediately.
Continue as usual; such small gaps even out by the end.
questions.explanation: Option D is incorrect. The right action is to halt, verify, document, and escalate - this is Option C. Continuing as usual hides an accounting risk. Personal reimbursement obscures the issue and violates transparency. "Adjusting" receipts is falsification. Therefore, Option C is correct because it preserves auditability and fixes the process in real time.

questions.question: Each question given below consists of a statement followed by two arguments numbered I and II, You have to decide which of the argument is a 'strong' argument and which is a 'weak' argument. Statement: Should all professional colleges in India be encouraged to run their own courses without affiliations to any university? Argument: I. Yes, this is the only way to create more opportunities for those who seek professional training. II. No, this will dilute the quality of professional training as all such colleges may not be equipped to conduct such courses.
options: If only argument I is strong.
If only argument II is strong.
If either I or II is strong.
If neither I nor II is strong
questions.explanation: Both the arguments are weak.

questions.question: If 7 # 5 = 144 and 12 # 1 = 169, then find the value of 16 # 6?
After removeing all vowels from the alphabet series, find the letter exactly midway between the 4th from the left and the 8th from the right.
questions.explanation: 4th from left => F (index 4) 8th from right => index 21 − 8 + 1 = 14 => R Mid-index => (4 + 14) ÷ 2 = 9 => L

Read the given statement and conclusions carefully. Decide which of the given conclusions is/are definitely true from the statement. Statement: W < C = X > Z ≥ B = R < A Conclusion I: Z ≥ A Conclusion II: B < C
options: Both conclusion I and conclusion II are true
Neither conclusion I nor conclusion II is true
questions.explanation: It is given that, W < C = X > Z ≥ B = R < A Conclusion I: Z ≥ A does not follow as the relationship between Z and A cannot be determined. Conclusion II: B < C follows as W < C = X > Z ≥ B = R < A. Hence, option (b) is correct.

questions.question: Consider the following events: 1) Kheda Satyagraha 2) Satyagraha Sabha 3) Champaran Satyagraha 4) Ahmedabad Mill Strike Arrange the following events in a chronological order and select the correct code from the options given below:
questions.explanation: · Champaran Satyagraha (1917) was the first civil disobedience movement for the problems of indigo planters of Champaran in Bihar. · Ahmedabad Mill Strike (February-March 1918) was the first Hunger Strike over the issue of discontinuation of plague bonus. · Kheda Satyagraha (March 1918) was the first Non-cooperation movement against the revenue code. · Satyagraha Sabha was formed in 1919 due to the failure of constitutional methods against the Rowlatt Act.

questions.question: Which of the following pairs is matched incorrectly in context to ancient India?
questions.explanation: - Crafts persons and merchants formed associations known as shrenis. - These shrenis of crafts persons provided training, procured raw material, and distributed the finished product. Further Insight: - In Ancient India, in northern part of the country, the village headman was known as the grama bhojaka. - Other independent farmers, known as grihapatis, most of whom were smaller landowners. - Men and Women, who did not own land, and had to earn a living working on the fields owned by othe1rs were knowns as Karmakara.

questions.question: The Comptroller and Auditor General (CAG) of India, who audits all government accounts, is appointed by the President and can only be removed on the same grounds and in the same manner as a __________.
questions.explanation: Article 148 states that the Comptroller and Auditor General (CAG) can only be removed from office in the same manner and on the same grounds as a Judge of the Supreme Court. Article 148: - Comptroller and Auditor-General of India. "There shall be a Comptroller and Auditor-General of India who shall be appointed by the President by warrant under his hand and seal and shall only be removed from office in like manner and on the like grounds as a Judge of the Supreme Court." - Article 149: Defines the duties and powers of the CAG, including auditing accounts of the Union, States, and other authorities as prescribed by law. - Article 150: Mandates that the accounts of the Union and States be kept in a form prescribed by the President on the advice of the CAG. - Article 151: Requires the CAG to submit audit reports on Union accounts to the President and on State accounts to the respective Governors, to be laid before Parliament or State Legislatures.

questions.question: Which of the following refers to the set of accounts that summarise a country's transaction with the rest of the world?
questions.explanation: - The Balance of Payments (BoP) of a country is the record of all economic transactions between the residents of the country and the rest of the world in a particular period of time (usually a year). - It includes all transactions involving goods, services, and financial assets. - These transactions are made by individuals, firms and government bodies. - The BoP is divided into two main accounts: the Current Account and the Capital Account. - The Current Account records the export and import of goods and services. a) It also includes income receipts and payments, such as wages and dividends, earned abroad or paid to foreigners. b) Additionally, it covers unilateral transfers, like gifts, donations, or remittances, which do not involve a direct exchange. - The Capital Account records the flow of capital, including investments and loans. a) It includes foreign direct investment (FDI), where foreigners invest in businesses or property in the country. b) It also covers portfolio investment, such as purchases of stocks and bonds by foreigners. c) Short-term capital flows, like bank loans or deposits, are also part of the Capital Account.

questions.question: Which of the following is subjects are covered under union list? A. Taxes on the consumption or sale of electricity B. Posts and telegraphs; telephones, wireless, broadcasting and other like forms of communication. C. Salaries and allowances of Ministers for the State. D. Railways
questions.explanation: Union List Subjects: · Defence · Army · International Relations · Ports · Railways · Highways · Communication · Posts and telegraphs; telephones, wireless, broadcasting and other like forms of communication. State List Subjects: · Public order · Police · Public health and sanitation · Hospitals and dispensaries · Betting and gambling · Taxes on the consumption or sale of electricity · Salaries and allowances of Ministers for the State

questions.question: Which of the following athletes is associated with shooting and won India's first individual Olympic gold medal?
questions.explanation: • Abhinav Bindra won India's first individual Olympic gold in shooting at the 2008 Beijing Olympics. • Vijender Singh is an Indian boxer. • P.V. Sindhu is a badminton player. • Milkha Singh was a renowned Indian sprinter. Further Insight: • Notable Indian Athletes: ○ Milkha Singh - Known as "The Flying Sikh." ○ P. T. Usha - India's "Golden Girl" in track and field. ○ Neeraj Chopra - Gold medalist in Javelin at the Tokyo Olympics 2020. ○ Anju Bobby George - Long jump star, first Indian to win a medal at World Championships. ○ Dutee Chand - Sprint specialist with national and international medals.

questions.question: Who appointed an Education Commission under Sir William Hunter in 1882?
questions.explanation: - Lord Ripon appointed the first Indian Education Commission under Sir William Hunter on February 3, 1882. - This Commission is popularly known as Hunter Commission. - The Hunter Commission was established to review and evaluate the state of education in India, particularly after the introduction of the English education system. - It aimed to assess the progress of primary and secondary education and suggest improvements.

questions.question: Which of the following elements is described as having properties intermediate between metals and nonmetals?
questions.explanation: Metalloids have properties intermediate between metals and non-metals. They can act as semiconductors under the right conditions, are solid at room temperature, and can form amphoteric oxides. Silicon is a metalloid, meaning it has properties that are intermediate between metals and nonmetals.

questions.question: Which unified portal launched in 2025 empowers the consumer and their advocates to file the consumer complaints along with payment of requisite fees online from anywhere for the redressal of their complaints?
questions.explanation: In a major boost to consumer rights, the e-Jagriti platform of the Department of Consumer Affairs has emerged as a transformative digital grievance redressal system, registering over two lakh users since its launch on January 1, 2025. The e-Jagriti portal empowers the consumer and their advocates to file the consumer complaints along with payment of requisite fees online from anywhere for the redressal of their complaints. The platform streamlines procedures for citizens by reducing paperwork, minimizing travel, and cutting down physical documentation, thereby promoting environmental sustainability. It also strengthens access for NRIs by removing geographical barriers and enabling them to assert their consumer rights from abroad.

questions.question: Match List I with List II and select the answer from the codes given below. States/UTLiteracy rate(Census 2011) 1. Kerala a.66.95% 2. Tamil Nadub.93.91% 3. Arunachal Pradeshc.80.33% 4. Lakshadweepd.92.28%
questions.explanation: As per provisional data of Census, 2011, literacy rates of some states/UT's are: Kerala - 93.91%; Tamil Nadu - 80.33%; Arunachal Pradesh - 66.95%; Lakshadweep - 92.28%.

questions.question: Consider the following statements and choose the correct statement(s) from the following: I. PM Street Vendor's AtmaNirbhar Nidhi (PM SVANidhi) was launched in 2020 to provide working capital loans to street vendors. II. It is implemented by the Ministry of Finance. III. The scheme provides collateral-free loans in multiple tranches with incentives for digital transactions.
questions.explanation: Statements I and III are correct, while Statement II is incorrect. Explanation of Correct Statements: Statement I is correct. The PM SVANidhi scheme was launched by the Government of India in June 2020 as part of the AtmaNirbhar Bharat package. Its primary objective was to provide affordable working capital loans to street vendors whose livelihoods were adversely affected by the COVID-19 lockdown. Statement III is correct. The scheme provides collateral-free loans, meaning vendors do not need to provide any security to avail of the loan. It is structured in multiple tranches: a first loan of up to ₹10,000, followed by subsequent loans of ₹20,000 and ₹50,000 upon successful repayment. The scheme also promotes digital transactions by offering a monthly cashback incentive. Explanation of Incorrect Statement: Statement II is incorrect. The PM SVANidhi scheme is implemented by the Ministry of Housing and Urban Affairs (MoHUA), not the Ministry of Finance. The Ministry of Finance is involved in the overall economic policy, but the nodal agency for this specific urban street vendor scheme is MoHUA. Further insights: The Small Industries Development Bank of India (SIDBI) is the technical partner for the implementation of the scheme and manages the credit guarantee to the lending institutions. The scheme has been a significant success in promoting financial inclusion and providing a safety net for one of the most vulnerable sections of the urban informal economy.

questions.question: On December 24, 2025, during the Vijay Hazare Trophy, which Indian cricketer became the fastest to reach 16,000 runs in List A cricket, a landmark that reset the pace benchmark for limited-overs domestic records?
questions.explanation: · India batting star Virat Kohli on December 24, 2025 went past the iconic Sachin Tendulkar as the fastest to reach 16,000 runs in List A cricket, achieving the milestone in his 330th innings in the ongoing Vijay Hazare Trophy. · Tendulkar had achieved the landmark in 391 innings. The 37-year-old Kohli touched the mark during his team Delhi's opening Vijay Hazare game against Andhra in Bangalore. at the BCCI Centre of Excellence. · Kohli now holds the record for being the fastest to each block of 1000 List A runs from 10,000 runs onwards. Kohli is playing the Vijay Hazare Trophy after a gap of 15 years, but the former India skipper has a stellar record in the tournament. · In the premier domestic one-day competition, he has made 819 runs from 13 matches at an average of 68.25 and a strike-rate of 106 with four centuries and three fifties.

questions.question: Which of the following is incorrect regarding Collenchyma?
options: Collenchyma generally occurs in hypodermis of dicot stem.
It is absent in the roots and also occurs in petioles and pedicels.
The cells are may be oval, polyhedral, cylindrical, irregular, elongated or armed.
The cell wall is unevenly thickened.
questions.explanation: Collenchyma is a simple, living mechanical tissue. Collenchyma generally occurs in hypodermis of dicot stem. It is absent in the roots and also occurs in petioles and pedicels. The cells are elongated and appear polygonal in cross section. The cell wall is unevenly thickened. It contains more of hemicellulose and pectin besides cellulose. It provides mechanical support and elasticity to the growing parts of the plant. Collenchyma consists of narrow cells. It has only a few small chloroplast or none. Tannin maybe present in collenchyma. Based on pattern of pectinisation of the cell wall, there are three types of collenchyma: Angular, Lamellar(Plate) and Lacunar(Tubular).

questions.question: Consider the following statements with regard to Money supply: I. M4 is more liquid than M1. II. The interbank deposits are not considered a part of money supply. III. M3 is the most commonly used measure of money supply. Which of the above statements is/are correct?
questions.explanation: RBI publishes figures for four alternative measures of money supply, viz. M1, M2, M3 and M4. They are defined as follows M1 = CU + DD M2 = M1 + Savings deposits with Post Office savings banks M3 = M1 + Net time deposits of commercial banks M4 = M3 + Total deposits with Post Office savings organisations (excluding National Savings Certificates) where, CU is currency (notes plus coins) held by the public and DD is net demand deposits held by commercial banks. The word 'net' implies that only deposits of the public held by the banks are to be included in money supply. Statement II is correct. The interbank deposits, which a commercial bank holds in other commercial banks, are not to be regarded as part of money supply. Statement I is incorrect. M1 and M2 are known as narrow money. M3 and M4 are known as broad money. These measures are in decreasing order of liquidity. M1 is most liquid and easiest for transactions whereas M4 is least liquid of all. Statement III is correct. M3 is the most commonly used measure of money supply. It is also known as aggregate monetary resources. Source: NCERT

questions.question: Identify the correct statement about Torgya festival of Arunachal Pradesh. I. It is celebrated by the Monpa community of Arunachal Pradesh. II. It is a three-day festival held in January every year. III. The first day of the festival begins by worshipping an image named "Torma".
questions.explanation: A festival to welcome healthy and a prosperous new year, Torgya festival is celebrated by the Monpa community of Arunachal Pradesh. This three-day festival is popularly celebrated during the first month of the year i.e January. It is held according to the Buddhist calendar days of 28th to 30th of Dawachukchipa (11th of the moon), which corresponds to 10 to 12 January of the Gregorian calendar. The first day of the festival begins by worshipping an image named "Torma". Making of the 3-feet tall and 2-feet wide effigy image begins 16 days before the festival, crafted by 14 Lamas (Monks). Four ingredients: butter, barley, milk, and molasses are used to make this tall effigy and take it to a particular location. After reaching the location, the Torma and put it in the burning Mechang (dried bamboo leaves). Post performing the ritual, they return to the monastery. This procession is performed with great pomp and show. The basic belief of the people is that the celebration of this festival is to eliminate any kind of external energy from the earth which can harm sentient beings and to protect them from natural calamities. This is a festival comprising oral and dance performances portraying the mythical events which are believed to have taken place in the past. The dances are performed by the monks from the monastery itself that requires strict training and discipline and a lot of practice before the programme. The distinct components of these performances are the colourful costumes and robes they wear while dancing. Many dance forms take place at this festival and are believed to be beneficial if observed in person. Two important dance (cham) forms are as follows: Pha Cham: The early morning dance takes place in the courtyard of Tawang monastery to purify the body of the people and spread good health and prosperity. Losjker Chungiye: performed by 12 monks for the 12 sun signs (Buddhist horoscope dance). Other dance forms: Palden Lhamo Cham Lham tsokor cham as Palden Lhamo's guards Durdak cham or Skeleton dance(protector of the graveyard)

questions.question: According to Demographic Transition theory, the first stage is characterized by
questions.explanation: - Demographic transition theory can be used to describe and predict the future population of any area. - The theory tells us that population of any region changes from high births and high deaths to low births and low deaths as society progresses from rural agrarian and illiterate to urban industrial and literate society - The first stage has high fertility and high mortality because people reproduce more to compensate for the deaths due to epidemics and variable food supply - Fertility remains high in the beginning of second stage but it declines with time. This is accompanied by reduced mortality rate. - In the last stage, both fertility and mortality decline considerably. The population is either stable or grows slowly. The population becomes urbanised, literate and has technical knowledge and controls the family size - ![](https://u1.oliveboard.in/exams/solution/oliveimg/p12862/image001.png) Source: NCERT

questions.question: As per Census of India 2011, which of the following state is the worst state with an incredibly low child sex ratio, followed by Punjab, Jammu & Kashmir, Delhi, Chandigarh, Uttarakhand and Himachal Pradesh?
questions.explanation: As per Census of India 2011, as many as nine States and Union Territories have a child sex ratio of under 900 females per 1000 males. · Haryana is the worst state with an incredibly low child sex ratio of 793 (the only state below 800), followed by Punjab, Jammu & Kashmir, Delhi, Chandigarh, Uttarakhand and Himachal Pradesh.

questions.question: As of 2022, which is the world's largest drainage basin with an area of about 70,00,000 sq.km.?
questions.explanation: The Amazon Basin is the world's largest drainage basin, with an area of about 7,000,000 square kilometers (2,700,000 sq mi). The Amazon Basin not only leads in area but also in discharge volume, making the Amazon River the largest by volume in the world. Major drainage basins of the world by area: Drainage Basin- Area (approx.)- Continent-Major River Amazon Basin - 7,000,000 km - South America - Amazon River Congo Basin - 4,000,000 km - Africa - Congo River Mississippi Basin - 3,200,000 km - North America - Mississippi River Nile Basin - 3,400,000 km - Africa - Nile River Ob-Irtysh Basin - 2,990,000 km - Asia (Russia) - Ob River Yenisei Basin - 2,580,000 km - Asia (Russia) - Yenisei River Lena Basin - 2,490,000 km - Asia (Russia) - Lena River Amur Basin - 1,855,000 km - Asia (Russia/China) - Amur River Paran Basin - 2,600,000 km - South America - Paran River

questions.question: Elelakkaradi dance of Kerala is performed by which of the following tribes?
questions.explanation: Elelakkaradi is a dance form depicts hunting of wild bear performed by Irula tribes. Whole community participates in the dance. One disguised as a wild bear and others sing and dance in a circle. In the song, they invite wild bear and perform actions of its chasing through the dance. When the bear runs away, the dance ends.

questions.question: One ____________ is equal to a flux that produces an electromotive force of one volt in a single turn of wire when the flux is uniformly reduced to zero in a period of one second.
questions.explanation: One weber is equal to a flux that produces an electromotive force of one volt in a single turn of wire when the flux is uniformly reduced to zero in a period of one second. The number of magnetic lines of forces set up in a magnetic circuit is called Magnetic Flux. It is analogous to electric current I in an electric circuit. Its SI unit is Weber (Wb).

questions.question: When was the Indian Hockey Federation established?
questions.explanation: The Indian Hockey Federation was founded in November 1925 in Gwalior. It served as the national governing body for field hockey in India until 2008. Key facts: - Played pivotal role in India's golden hockey era (1928-1956 Olympics) - Replaced by Hockey India in 2009 after administrative disputes - Original IHF was affiliated to FIH (International Hockey Federation)

questions.question: Under which condition/conditions a member of parliament can be disqualified? I. If he holds office of profit under Government of India II. If disqualified by any law. III. If he is an undischarged insolvent
questions.explanation: The constitution of India has provided (in article 102) that a member of parliament will be disqualified for membership if: - He holds any office of profit under the Union or state government (except that of a minister or any other office exempted by Parliament) - He is of unsound mind and stands so declared by a court. - He is an undischarged insolvent. - He has ceased to be a citizen of India. - He is disqualified under any other law by parliament

questions.question: Consider the following statements: I. The ratio of Real GDP to Nominal GDP is known as GDP Deflator. II. GDP deflator value is published by the Ministry of Commerce and Industry. Which of the above statements is/are correct?
questions.explanation: Statement I is incorrect. The ratio of nominal to real GDP is known as GDP Deflator. It is a well-known index of prices which measures Inflation. Statement II is incorrect. The National Statistical Office (NSO) of the Ministry of statistics and Programme Implementation (MOSPI) releases data of GDP and GDP deflator. Source: NCERT

questions.question: Maha Shivaratri is celebrated in which month of Hindu Calendar?
questions.explanation: According to Hindu calendar, Maha Shivratri the festival is observed on the fourteenth (Chaturdashi) day of the dark half of the lunar month of Phalguna or Magha. Maha Shivaratri, the festival honouring Lord Shiva, the supreme deity of destruction and regeneration, is celebrated annually between February and March. Further insights: Holi is a Hindu festival celebrated in the month of Phalgun, which usually falls in February or March. Diwali is celebrated in the month of Kartika (between mid October and mid November). It gets its name from the Sanskrit word Deepawali which means rows of lights. Diwali symbolizes the victory of good over evil. Raksha Bandhan is observed on the last day of the Hindu lunar calendar month of Shravana, which typically falls in August.

questions.question: Read the Assertion (A)and Reason (R) and choose the correct option Assertion (A): Sponges (Porifera) lack true tissues and organs. Reason (R): Sponges are diploblastic animals with distinct ectoderm and endoderm layers.
options: Both A and R are true, and R is the correct explanation of A
Both A and R are true, but R is not the correct explanation of A
A is true, but R is false
A is false, but R is true
questions.explanation: Sponges indeed lack true tissues; they have cellular-level organization. They are not diploblastic; sponges do not form germ layers like ectoderm and endoderm, so the Reason is false. Further Insights: - Porifera possess choanocytes (collar cells) that drive water flow and capture food. - Skeletal elements are spicules (CaCO3 or silica) and/or spongin fibers. - Canal systems: asconoid, syconoid, leuconoid (increasing complexity). - Most sponges are asymmetrical; some show radial symmetry. - Freshwater genus Spongilla; marine genus Sycon are standard examples used in exams.

questions.question: The average weight of 24 students in a class is 40 kg. if the weight of the teacher is included, the average is increased by 500 gms. Find The weight of the teacher?
questions.explanation: 25*40.5 - 24*40 = 52.5 Hence, the answer is option (2).

questions.question: After dividing a number successively by 5, 7, and 11, the remainder are 2, 3, and 4. What will be the remainder, if 15 divides the same number (if the last quotient is 1)?
questions.explanation: The last quotient is 1. The number that is divided by 11 = 11 x 1 + 4 = 15 The number that is divided by 7 = 15 x 7 + 3 = 108 The number that is divided by 5 = 108 x 5 + 2 = 542 When we divide 542 by 15, we get 2 as a remainder.

questions.question: If p = √7 + 1_________√7 - 1 and q = √7 - 1_________√7 + 1 , then find the value of p² + pq + q²_________p² -pq + q²
Find x if: √(15 + x) + √(15 - x)_________√(15 + x) - √(15 - x) = 3
questions.explanation: Let a = √(15 + x) and b = √(15 - x) Then, the equation becomes, (a + b)/(a - b) = 3 a + b = 3a - 3b 2a = 4b a = 2b √(15 + x) = 2[√(15 - x)] 15 + x = 4 (15 - x) 15 + x = 60 - 4x 5x = 45 x = 9

questions.question: The number of 50 paise coins is 3 times that of ₹1 coins, and the number of ₹2 coins is half the number of ₹1 coins. If the total value is ₹217, then find the number of ₹1 coins.
questions.explanation: Let the number of Rs. 1coins be x Number of 50 paise coins = 3x Number of Rs. 2 coins = x/2 According to question, x * 1 + 3x * 0.5 + x/2 * 2 = 217 x + 3x/2 + x = 217 7x/2 = 217 x = 62

questions.question: A rectangular board is divided into four sections along with the breadth, such that the widths of the sections form an arithmetic progression, starting with 5 cm width for first section. The length of a rectangular board is 7 cm more than 5/3 of the breadth of the board. If the area of the rectangular board is 480 cm², then find the width of largest section.
questions.explanation: Let length of rectangular board is '5X + 7' cm and '3X' cm respectively. Area of the rectangular board = 480 cm² 3X(5X + 7) = 480 5X² + 7X - 160 = 0 5X² - 25X + 32X - 160 = 0 (X - 5)(5X + 32) = 0 X = 5, -32/5 Length = 5*5 + 7 = 32 cm a = 5, n = 4, d = ? Sum of AP = n/2 * [2a + (n - 1)d] 4/2 * [2*5 + (4 - 1)*d] = 32 10 + 3d = 16 3d = 6 d = 2 Width of largest section = a + 3d = 5 + 3*2 = 11 cm

questions.question: Uma is 10 years younger than Rama. Average of present age of Rama and Uma is equal to the age of Huma 6 years before. If the ratio of Rama after 3 years and Huma 8 years ago is 3:2 respectively, then find the present age of Huma.
questions.explanation: Let present age of Uma = 't' years Present age of Rama = t + 10 Present age of Huma = (t + t + 10)/2 + 6 = t + 11 Now, (t + 10 + 3)/(t + 11 - 8) = 3/2 2t + 26 = 3t + 9 t = 17 So, the present age of Huma = 17 + 11 = 28 years

questions.question: In a 105-litre mixture of syrup and water, the ratio is 2 : 5 respectively. How much water should be added so that the ratio becomes 1 : 4?
questions.explanation: Initial syrup = 105 X (2/7) => 30 litres Initial water = 105 − 30 => 75 litres Let x = water added Required ratio: 30 : (75 + x) = 1 : 4 => 30/(75 + x) = ¼ => 120 = 75 + x => x = 45 litres

questions.question: If M is the point on line segment PQ such that PM = MQ and coordinates of P, Q and M are (4,7, 5), (6,5,7) and (a, b, c), then what is the sum of a, b and c?
questions.explanation: As per given information M is the midpoint of the line segment PQ So, according to the mid-point formula M (a, b, c) = (((4 +6)/2), ((7 +5)/2), ((5 +7)/2)) = (5,6,6) Required sum = 5 +6 +6 = 17

questions.question: A and B together can complete a gardening task in 12 days. They work for 4 days together, and then B continues alone and finishes the rest of the task in 12 more days. How many days would A alone require to complete the entire task?
questions.explanation: Rate (A + B) = 1/12 => work in 4 days = ⅓ => remaining = ⅔. => B's rate = (⅔)/12 = 1/18 => A's rate = 1/12 - 1/18 = 1/36 => A takes 36 days.

questions.question: Pawan covers 1.5 km in 15 minutes and Naman beats Pawan by 150 m, then find the time taken by Naman to completer the race of 1.5 km?
questions.explanation: Time taken by Pawan = 15 minutes Naman beats Pawan by 150 m. Distance covered by Pawan = 1.5 km - 150 m = 1500 - 150 = 1350 m Time taken by Naman to completer the race of 1.5 km = 1350 * 15/1500 = 13.5 minutes

questions.question: An aeroplane when 3900 m high passes vertically above another aeroplane at an instance when their angles of elevation at the same observation point are 45° and 30° respectively. How many meters is one plane higher than other?
questions.explanation: ![](https://u1.oliveboard.in/exams/solution/oliveimg/p17132/image004.jpg) Let plane A be h meter above plane B. In Δ ACD, Tan45° = AC/CD 1 = 3900/x x = 3900 m Δ BCD, Tan30° = BC/CD (1/√3) = (3900 - h)/3900 3900√3 - √3h = 3900 h = 3900 * (√3 - 1)/√3 h = 1300√3(√3 - 1) m

questions.question: If ![](https://u1.oliveboard.in/exams/solution/oliveimg/p6988/image007.png) =13/20, then what is the value of 'x'?

Sam completed a work in 36 days and Veer is 80% more efficient than Sam. They start working together, but Veer left the work after 9 days due to some issue, then Sam alone can complete the remaining work in:
questions.explanation: Sam alone completed the work in 36 days. Thus, Veer alone completed the work in = 36 * 100/180 = 20 days In 9 days, part of work done by Sam and Veer = 9 * (1/36 + 1/20) = 7/10 So, remaining work = 1 - 7/10 = 3/10 Hence, Sam alone can complete the remaining work in = 3/10 * 36 = 10.8 days

questions.question: HL is the diameter of a circle and G is point on circle such that KG ⊥ HL where K is a point on HL. If radius of the circle is 21 cm and GL = 36 cm, then what is the length of KL?
questions.explanation: ![](https://u1.oliveboard.in/exams/solution/oliveimg/p15543/image016.jpg) In ∆GHL, cos L = Base/Hypotenuse = GL/HL And, In ∆GKL, cos L = Base/Hypotenuse = KL/GL So, KL/GL = GL/HL => KL/36 = 36/(2 * 21) => KL = 36/42 * 36 => KL = 216/7 => KL = 30(6/7) cm

questions.question: A sum of ₹5000 is borrowed by Chhavi for one year at 32% per annum compounded half-yearly. Find the compound interest.

Two trains - A and B having the lengths 205 m and L m respectively, are running in the same direction on parallel lines. Speeds of A and B are 67 km/hr and 80 km/hr respectively. Find the value of L if time taken by them to cross each other is 108 seconds.
questions.explanation: Total distance covered = (205 + L) m Relative speed of train B with respect to A = 80 - 67 = 13 km/hr = (13 * 5/18) m/sec According to the question, 205 + L = (13 * 5/18) * 108 205 + L = 390 L = 185 m

questions.question: If M_________30 - M + N_________24 - N + O_________18 - O = 8, then find 5_________30 - M + 4_________24 - N + 3_________18 - O
questions.explanation: ![](https://u1.oliveboard.in/exams/solution/oliveimg/p23849/image027.png) ![](https://u1.oliveboard.in/exams/solution/oliveimg/p23849/image028.png) ![](https://u1.oliveboard.in/exams/solution/oliveimg/p23849/image029.png) ![](https://u1.oliveboard.in/exams/solution/oliveimg/p23849/image030.png) ![](https://u1.oliveboard.in/exams/solution/oliveimg/p23849/image031.png)

questions.question: The sides of a triangle are 4 cm, 6 cm and 8 cm. What is the difference between the circumradius and inradius of the triangle?
questions.explanation: Semi-perimeter = (4 + 6 + 8)/2 = 9 cm Area = √(9(9 - 4)(9 - 6)(9 - 8)) = 3√15 cm Circumradius = (4*6*8)/(4*3√15) = 16/√15 cm Inradius = 3√15/9 = √15/3 cm Required difference = 16/√15 − √15/3 = 11/√15 cm

questions.question: Ram purchased a flat at Rs.1 lakh and Prem purchase a plot of land worth Rs.1.1 lakh . The respective annual rates at which the price of the flat and the plot increased were 10% and 5%. After two years they exchanged their property and one paid the other the difference. Then…..
questions.explanation: " Ram’s flat value after 2 years = 1(1+0.1) ² =1.1 ² Prem’s plot value after 2 years = 1.1 * (1 + 0.05)² = 1.1 * (1.05)² Difference = 1.1 * (1.05)² - 1.1² = 1.1 * (1.1025 - 1.1) = 1.1 * 0.0025 lakh = 1.1 * 0.0025 * 100000 = Rs.275 Ram paid Rs.275 to Prem"

questions.question: In the given figure, ADEF is a rectangle. ∠CBE = 70°, find the value of X. ![](https://u1.oliveboard.in/exams/solution/oliveimg/p15211/image015.jpg)
questions.explanation: Each interior angle of rectangle = 90° Therefore, ∠BAC = 90 - 40 = 50° Now, in triangle ABC, ∠BCA = 180 - (50 + X) = (130 - X)° Now, AD || FE and BC is transversal line. So, ∠CBE = ∠BCA [alternate interior angles] 70 = 130 - X X = 130 - 70 X = 60°

questions.question: The cost price of 12 books is equal to the selling price of 8 books. Find the profit percentage.
questions.explanation: Let CP of each book be C. CP of 8 books = 8C SP of 8 books = CP of 12 books = 12C Profit % = 4/8 x 100 = 50 %

questions.question: A and B enters into partnership with investment of Rs. 8000 and Rs. 6000 respectively. If A invest for a year and they divide the annual profit in the ratio of 8 : 3 then for how many months B invest the money?
questions.explanation: Let the time for which B invest = x months According to question, (8000 * 12) : (6000 * x ) = 8 : 3 (8 * 6) : (3 * x) = 8 : 3 16/x = 8/3 x = 6 months

questions.question: If X = 1_________sin² A + 1_________cos² A , find the value of √X
questions.explanation: " ![](https://u1.oliveboard.in/exams/solution/oliveimg/stubeimg/SSCT-MML-006484.png) "

questions.question: Kavya invested Rs. 25000, Arnav invested Rs. 35000, and Sanya invested Rs. 45000 at the start of a venture. At the end of 4 months, Arnav withdrew Rs. 5000 and Sanya added Rs. 15000. After 8 months, Kavya withdrew Rs. 10000 and Kabir joined with Rs. 30000 for the remaining period. If the total profit at the end of the year was Rs. 142000, what was Kabir's share?
questions.explanation: Kavya's contribution * Time = 25000 X 8 + 15000 X 4 = Rs. 260000 Arnav's contribution * Time = 35000 X 4 + 30000 X 8 = Rs. 380000 Sanya's contribution * Time = 45000 X 4 + 60000 X 8 = Rs. 660000 Kabir's contribution * Time = 30000 X 4 = Rs. 120000 The ratio of profit share = 260000: 380000: 660000: 120000 = 13: 19: 33: 6 Hence, Kabir's profit share = 6/71 X 142000 = Rs. 12000

questions.question: Select the option that expresses the given sentence in reported speech. The instructor said, "An ounce of prevention is worth a pound of cure."
options: The instructor said that an ounce of prevention was worth a pound of cure.
The instructor said that an ounce of prevention is worth a pound of cure.
The instructor said that an ounce of prevention had been worth a pound of cure.
The instructor said that an ounce of prevention has been worth a pound of cure.
questions.explanation: When a Direct Speech contains an idiom or phrase, there should be no change while transforming it into Indirect Speech. For example: The minister said, "The mice will play when the cat is away." The minister said that the mice will play when the cat is away. The following things in the sentence should change when changed from Direct speech to Indirect Speech: Direct SpeechIndirect Speech saidsaid that Therefore, the correct answer is: (b) The instructor said that an ounce of prevention is worth a pound of cure.

questions.question: Choose the correct meaning of the idiom: Has two strings to his bow
options: Possesses influence over two powerful groups
Succeeds through luck rather than skill
Has an additional skill, option, or resource to rely on
questions.explanation: "Has two strings to his bow" refers to having an additional skill, alternative option, or extra resource that can be used if the first fails. Option (d) captures this idea of backup capability. Options (a), (b), and (c) do not reflect the idiom's meaning of maintaining multiple competencies or alternatives. Hence, option (d) is the correct answer.

questions.question: Choose the word from the options that is most similar in meaning to the given word. Baffled
questions.explanation: 'Baffled' means '(Adj) Extremely confused or puzzled'. Options a) and c) can be rejected as they are unrelated to the word. 'Enlightened' means 'Having or showing a rational, modern, and well-informed outlook.'. Hence, option b) can also be rejected as it is the exact opposite. 'Haughty' means 'Arrogantly superior and disdainful.'. 'Peripheral' means 'Relating to or situated on the edge or periphery of something.'. Hence, option d) is the correct answer.

questions.question: Select the option that expresses the given sentence in active voice. The rare binoculars were gifted to the museum by the explorer.
options: The museum gifted the rare binoculars to the explorer.
The explorer gifted the rare binoculars to the museum.
The rare binoculars gifted the explorer to the museum.
The explorer gifts the rare binoculars to the museum.
questions.explanation: The subject of the Passive Voice is changed into the object of the Active Voice. Also, the object of the Passive Voice is changed into the subject of the Active Voice. The removal of the "to be" ['were'] verb and the preposition "by" is necessary while changing the voice of the sentence from Passive to Active. Plural nouns (e.g., binoculars) retain their form in Active Voice. Examples of such nouns: Binoculars, trousers, pants, alms, archives, fireworks, scissors, tongs, pliers, surroundings, troops, tactics, valuables, belongings, ashes, assets. The sentence in the Passive voice is in Simple Past tense. So, the sentence in the Active voice should be in Simple Past tense. Passive format: Object (Plural) + was/were + V3 + by + Subject. Active format: Subject + V2 + Object (Plural). For example: Trousers were given to me by him. (Passive) He gave me trousers. (Active) Only option b) matches the Past Indefinite structure. Correct answer: b) The explorer gifted the rare binoculars to the museum. Note: V1 -> Base form/simple form of verb, V2 -> Past form of verb (-ed form or irregular verb), V3 -> Past participle form of verb (-ed, -d, -t, -en, or -n), V4 -> Continuous form of verb / Present participle form of verb (-ing form), V5 -> Third person singular (V1+ -es or -s).

questions.question: Rearrange the following sentences to form a coherent paragraph: 1) Fake reviews can make an ordinary product appear trustworthy. 2) These days online shoppers often decide within minutes by scanning ratings and short comments. 3) To counter this, platforms increasingly use verification badges and detect unusual reviewing patterns. 4) Over time, fake reviews manipulation reduces confidence and pushes buyers to doubt even genuine feedback.
questions.explanation: Correct Option: D gives 4 → 2 → 1 → 3. Sentence 4 states the long-term problem (confidence reduces). Sentence 2 explains why reviews matter (fast decisions). Sentence 1 shows the manipulation that exploits this (fake reviews). Sentence 3 gives the counter-action (to counter this, platforms..). Rule used (simple): Problem → why it matters → what causes it → what is done about it. Example: "Road accidents rise → people travel daily → speeding causes crashes → police add speed cameras." · Option A is wrong because it gives "counter this" (3) only at the end but the "this" becomes too delayed and less clear after multiple ideas. · Option B is wrong because it ends with the negative long-term result (4) after the solution (3); it weakens the closing. · Option C is wrong because it places "manipulation reduces confidence" (4) before clearly establishing the manipulation mechanism (1) and the decision habit (2) in a connected way. Final correct paragraph: 4 → 2 → 1 → 3. Hence, option (4) is correct.

questions.question: In the following question, the first and the last parts of the sentence/passage are numbered 1 and 6. The rest of the sentence/passage is split into four parts - P, Q, R, and S. These four parts are not given in their proper order. Read the sentence/passage and find the most logical order of the parts between 1 and 6. 1: The printing press revolutionized the spread of knowledge in early modern Europe. P. Books and pamphlets could now be reproduced in large quantities. Q. This innovation drastically reduced the time required to create written materials. R. As printing became cheaper, literacy rates began to rise across several regions. S. Before its invention, manuscripts had to be copied by hand, which was slow and expensive. 6: Its impact laid the foundation for scientific, cultural, and political transformations.
questions.explanation: The passage describes how the printing press changed information dissemination. 1 introduces the invention. S is the natural starting point, describing the situation before the press existed. Q follows by contrasting S with the rapid production enabled by printing. P then explains mass reproduction as a direct result of Q. R describes the social outcome - rising literacy. 6 concludes with broader consequences. Thus, the correct and logical sequence is SQPR. Hence, option (c) is the correct answer.

questions.question: Select the INCORRECTLY spelt word.
questions.explanation: Option (b) is the correct answer. The correct spelling is "consensus". The other options are spelled correctly. Conscious: aware of one's thoughts and surroundings. Consensus: a general agreement. Conscientious: diligent and thorough in one's work. Conscience: the inner sense of what is right or wrong.

questions.question: Select the option that will improve the underlined part of the given sentence. In case no improvement is needed, select 'No improvement required'. An immense crowd had gather to witness this ceremony.
questions.explanation: The past tense construction to be used here is (had + third form of the verb), which always uses the past participle form of the verb. Here, it is 'gathered'. 'Gather' [base verb] and 'gathering' [-ing verb] are negated. For C [gather - plural] to be correct, the singular form of the verb 'gathers' should have been used, as 'crowd' is a single entity. B is the right answer. Note: A participle is a verb form used as an adjective or to create verb tenses. There are two main types of participles: Present Participle: Formed by adding "-ing" to the base form of a verb (e.g., running, singing). Past Participle: Usually ends in -ed, -d, -t, -en, or -n (e.g., broken, cooked). Examples: The barking dog (present participle). The broken vase (past participle).

questions.question: Select the option that can be used as a one-word substitute for the given group of words. Relating to old people
questions.explanation: Geriatric - Relating to old people, especially with regard to their healthcare. Paediatric - Relating to the branch of medicine dealing with children and their diseases. Bariatric - The branch of medicine that deals with the study and treatment of obesity. Allopatric - (of animals or plants, especially of related species or populations) occurring in separate non-overlapping geographical areas. So, Option B is the right answer.

questions.question: Select the correct Synonym of "INIMICAL".
questions.explanation: "Inimical" means harmful or hostile. (a) Friendly = kind (b) Uncertain = not sure (c) Hostile = aggressive (d) Curation = the action or process of selecting, organizing Only (c) matches. Hence, option (c) is the answer.

questions.question: Select the most appropriate ANTONYM of the given word. Venerate
questions.explanation: The word "venerate" means to respect or honour deeply. Its common synonyms are revere, admire, and worship. Option A is correct because "despise" means to hate or look down on someone or something. To venerate is to hold in very high respect. To despise is to feel strong dislike or contempt. So, "despise" is the opposite of "venerate". Option A means hate or regard with contempt. Option B means look up to with approval. Option C means show regard. Option D means someone having experience. Therefore, the correct answer is Option A.

questions.question: Select the most appropriate option to fill in the blank. The vehicles ________ in queue for a very long time.
questions.explanation: The blank requires a verb that indicates the present continuous tense because it can be seen that the event is still happening and not yet over. 'Is waiting' and 'was waiting' correspond to singular nouns, but 'vehicles' is a plural noun, so they are negated. 'Are waited' is an incorrect verb form since it is in the passive voice, while a verb needs to be in the active voice since the "vehicles" are the performers of the action, so it is also negated. 'Are waiting' is the most appropriate active verb that is in the present continuous tense form, which indicates that the action still continues and also matches the context of the sentence. Hence, the correct answer is option (b). The correct sentence is: The vehicles are waiting in a queue for a very long time. Note: Active and Passive Voice: When the person doing the action is the subject, the sentence is said to be in active form/voice. When the subject is what the action is directed at, then the sentence is said to be in passive form/voice. For example: Active Voice: Rita and her friends performed a skit. Here, 'Rita and her friends' (subject) perform the action. Passive Voice: A skit was performed by Rita and her friends. Here, 'skit' is the subject (at which the action is directed).

questions.question: Select the most appropriate pronoun to fill in the blank. Sam is so furious that people consider Jenna to be smarter than ________.
questions.explanation: A pronoun directly after 'than' or 'as' is usually in the accusative case (The "accusative case" is used when the noun is the direct object in the sentence.) unless there is a verb after it. If a verb follows it, the nominative form is used. No verb follows the blank. So, we eliminate the options which are pronouns in the nominative (subjective) case. So, options b) and d) are incorrect. Comparison is between Jenna and Sam only. The pronoun must replace Sam (one person). 'Them' is plural, so we are left with 'him' [singular] only to fill in the blank. Hence, option a) is correct.

questions.question: Choose the word that is opposite in meaning to the given word. Sporadic
questions.explanation: The word "sporadic" means happening at irregular times and not continuously. Its common synonyms are occasional, irregular, and scattered. Option C is correct because "steady" means regular, continuous, and even. Something sporadic happens here and there, but something steady happens in a regular manner. So, "steady" is the opposite of "sporadic". Option A means not frequent. Option B means happening sometimes only. Option C means regular and continuous. Option D means spread out or not closely grouped. Therefore, the correct answer is Option C.

questions.question: Select the most appropriate option to fill in the blank no. 1.
questions.explanation: The sentence discusses the promise of biotechnology in transforming agriculture towards sustainability. The word "immense" means extremely large or vast. In the context of the sentence, it suggests that biotechnology holds great potential or vast promise in transforming agriculture. Diminutive - This word means extremely small or tiny, which is the opposite of what is being conveyed regarding the potential of biotechnology in agriculture. Minuscule - This word also means extremely small or insignificant, which is not appropriate given the significant potential of biotechnology in agriculture. Hence, option (a) is correct.

questions.question: Select the most appropriate option to fill in the blank no. 2.
questions.explanation: The word "elucidating" means to make something clear or understandable, especially by explaining it in more detail. In the context of the sentence, "elucidating molecular mechanisms" suggests that recent advancements in biotechnology research are aimed at clarifying and understanding the intricate details of molecular mechanisms involved in agricultural processes. "Muddling" means to confuse. No other word can fit in the blank contextually. Hence, option (d) is correct.

questions.question: Select the most appropriate option to fill in the blank no. 3.
questions.explanation: The sentence discusses various biotechnological tools that play a key role in sustainable farming practices. The word "hold" in this context means to maintain or possess a significant position or importance. In the sentence, it suggests that these biotechnological tools are crucial or essential for sustainable farming practices. "Relinquish" means to give up or surrender. No other word can fit in the blank contextually. Hence, option (c) is correct.

questions.question: Select the most appropriate option to fill in the blank no. 4.
questions.explanation: "Done" here implies that the adoption of biotechnology in the seed industry was an action that was accomplished or executed. It indicates that the process of integrating biotechnology into the seed industry took place in the past. Preferred - This word means to like or favour one thing over another, which doesn't accurately describe the process of adoption of biotechnology in the seed industry. Polished - This word means to improve or refine something, which doesn't fit the context of discussing the initial adoption of biotechnology in the seed industry. Hence, option (a) is correct.

questions.question: Select the most appropriate option to fill in the blank no. 5.
questions.explanation: "Fuelled" fits well with the context of advancements and progress in biotechnology research, indicating that understanding molecular mechanisms has been driving or fuelling research in the past few decades. Hence, option (d) is correct. Inert - lacking the power to move.

questions.question: Select the option that can be used as a one-word substitute for the given group of words. A person with strange habits.
questions.explanation: 'Eccentric' means a person with strange habits. Hence, (d) is the correct answer. 'Foolish' means lacking good sense or judgement. 'Ignorant' means lacking knowledge or awareness in general. 'Lousy' means terrible or disgusting.

questions.question: Select the option that expresses the given sentence in direct speech. Shreya said that she was having a sandwich for her breakfast.
options: Shreya said, "She was having a sandwich for her breakfast."
Shreya said, "I am having a sandwich for my breakfast."
Shreya said, "She will be having a sandwich for her breakfast."
Shreya said, "I was having a sandwich for my breakfast."
questions.explanation: The sentence in the Indirect Speech is in Past Continuous Tense. Therefore, the sentence in the Direct Speech will be in Present Continuous Tense. Past Continuous (Subject + was/were + V1 + ing + Object) will be converted into Present Continuous (Subject + is/am/are + V1 +ing + Object) For example: He said that he was checking the assignment. (Indirect Speech) He said, "I am checking the assignment." (Direct Speech) The following things in the sentence will change when changed from Indirect Speech to Direct Speech: She - I Was - am Her - my Hence, (b) is the correct answer.

questions.question: The following sentence has been split into three segments. Identify the segment that contains a grammatical error. If the sentence is error-free, mark 'No error' as your answer. He opposed vehemently / the construction of / the dam across the river.
questions.explanation: The part mentioned in option (c) has the error. An adverb is either used before the transitive verb or at the end of the clause but in the sentence, the adverb 'vehemently' is incorrectly used after the transitive verb 'opposed'. The correct sentence is: He vehemently opposed the construction of the dam across the river. Hence, the correct answer is option (c). Note: Qualifiers: A word or phrase that limits the meaning of another word or phrase or makes it less general. 'Adverbs' and 'adjectives' are qualifiers. Transitive and intransitive verbs: Some verbs always need an object (a noun, phrase, or pronoun that refers to the person or thing that is affected by the action of the verb). These are called transitive verbs. Some verbs never have an object. These are called intransitive verbs. Examples: 1. Transitive verb: "Do you believe in miracles"; the boldened part is the transitive verb and the underlined part is the object. 2. Intransitive verb: "It was snowing heavily."; here 'snowing' is the intransitive verb (contains no object).

questions.question: Choose the most suitable option to replace the highlighted part of the sentence. You should return the report earlier if you wanted it to be useful.
options: should had returning the report earlier
ought to have return the report earlier
should have returned the report earlier
questions.explanation: The grammar concept of "modal perfect forms for past obligation or reproach" applies here. The highlighted part is wrong because "should return" expresses a present obligation, but the sentence refers to a past failure ("earlier"). Past judgments require the modal perfect form modal + have + past participle, which expresses criticism of something that did not happen. Option (a) - incorrect Violates modal structure: a modal cannot be followed by "had." The participle "returning" further breaks the pattern. Option (b) - incorrect After "have," the verb must be a past participle ("returned"), not the base form. Option (c) - incorrect Modals always take a bare infinitive, so "returns" is ungrammatical after "should." Option (d) - correct Uses the full modal perfect "should have returned," which expresses reproach for a past action that did not occur. Hence, option (d) is correct. Note: Modal perfects are essential for referring to unrealised past obligations: should have + past participle ought to have + past participle could have / would have (for possibility or hypothesis)

questions.question: Select the sentence containing the homonym of the highlighted word: The tired bear began to lumber back to its den.
options: The company processed logs into lumber at the sawmill.
He watched the exhausted player lumber off the field.
The old truck continued to lumber up the steep hill.
The giant figure started to lumber towards the frightened villagers.
questions.explanation: A homonym is a word that is spelled and pronounced the same as another word but has a different meaning. In the original sentence, "lumber" is a verb that means to move in a slow, heavy, or awkward way. In options (b), (c), and (d), "lumber" is also used as a verb with the exact same meaning. In option (a), "lumber" is a noun that refers to timber or logs that have been prepared for use. This is a completely different meaning, making it the correct homonym. Hence, option (a) is the correct answer.

questions.question: Identify the sentence that uses the superlative degree of comparison.
options: Of the two sisters, Rhea is more patient than Neha during stressful situations.
Rhea is patient and composed when dealing with stressful situations.
Rhea becomes more patient with every stressful situation she handles.
Rhea is the most patient member of her entire family during stressful situations.
questions.explanation: The correct sentence is option d). "The most patient" is the superlative degree, used when comparing Rhea with more than two people in the entire family. Option a) uses the comparative form "more patient," which is used for comparing two people. Option b) uses "patient" in the positive degree, with no comparison. Option c) uses "more patient" as a comparative indicating gradual improvement, not a superlative form. Note: · The Positive Degree is used to denote the mere existence of some quality of what we speak about. It is used when no comparison is made. · The Comparative degree denotes a higher degree of quality than the positive and is used when two things are compared. · The Superlative degree indicates the highest degree of quality and is used when more than two things (or sets of things) are compared.
